title: Shri Navgrah Aarti
intro: Read Shri Navgrah Aarti in Hindi and seek the divine blessings of the Navgrahas.
description:
Aarti Shri Navgrahon ki keejai,
Badha, kasht, rog, har leejai॥
Surya tej vyape jeevan bhar,
Jaki kripa kabhoot nahin cheejai॥
Roop Chandra sheetalta layein,
Shanti sneh saras rasu bheejai॥
Mangal hare amangal sara,
Saumya sudha ras amrit peejai॥
Budh sada vaibhav yash leeye,
Sukh sampati Lakshmi paseejai॥
Vidya buddhi gyan Guru se le lo,
Pragati sada manav pai reejhe॥
Shukra tark vigyan badhavein,
Desh dharm seva yash leejai॥
Nyayadhish Shani ati jyare,
Jap tap shraddha Shani ko deejai॥
Rahu man ka bharam harave,
Saath na kabahu kukarm na deejai॥
Swasthya uttam Ketu rakhai,
Paradheenata manhit kheejai॥

faq.q1: What is the main significance of performing Navgrah Aarti?
faq.a1: It is performed to appease the malefic effects of planetary positions, bring positive energy into life, and remove astrological defects (doshas).
faq.q2: Which is the most auspicious day for Navgrah worship and Aarti?
faq.a2: While it can be done on any day, Sundays (for the Sun) or the specific day associated with the respective planet are considered especially fruitful.
faq.q3: What should be offered during the Aarti?
faq.a3: Offering various grains, black sesame seeds, jaggery, and mustard oil according to the respective planets is considered auspicious.
faq.q4: Can Navgrah Aarti be performed at home?
faq.a4: Yes, performing the Aarti with proper rituals and purity in front of a Navgrah Yantra or idol is highly auspicious.
faq.q5: What should be done after the Aarti for peace of mind?
faq.a5: Reciting the 'Navgrah Stotram' is considered the best practice for mental and spiritual peace.
